title: Nursery Rhyme. LXXVIII. Tales.
content:
Our saucy boy Dick,
        Had a nice little stick
            Cut from a hawthorn tree;
        And with this pretty stick,
        He thought he could beat
            A boy much bigger than he.

        But the boy turned round,
        And hit him a rebound,
            Which did so frighten poor Dick,
        That, without more delay,
        He ran quite away,
            And over a hedge he jumped quick.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. LXXVIII. Tales."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
